task_completion.description: The Anterior Commissure (AC) is identified with a fiducial point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the sagittal MRI view, a fiducial point labeled 'AC' (or similar) is visible at the anterior commissure (junction of the lamina terminalis and rostrum of corpus callosum, anterior to the fornix).

task_completion.description: The Posterior Commissure (PC) is identified with a fiducial point.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the sagittal MRI view, a fiducial point labeled 'PC' (or similar) is visible at the posterior commissure (junction of the cerebral aqueduct and third ventricle, just below the pineal gland).

task_completion.description: A line markup connects the AC and PC points.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A visible line segment connects the AC and PC fiducials in the 2D slice view or 3D view. The AC point appears anterior (forward) relative to the PC point.

task_completion.description: The AC-PC distance is verified to be within the physiological range (19-32mm).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Markups module panel or a measurement label in the view displays the length of the line, showing a value between approximately 19.0 mm and 32.0 mm (typically ~25 mm).

task_completion.description: The markups are saved to the correct JSON file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save' dialog or file browser is visible showing the filename 'ACPC_landmarks.mrk.json' being saved to the '~/Documents/SlicerData/Exports/' directory (or similar path).

integrity.description: The agent interacts with the 3D Slicer GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 3D Slicer application window, toolbars, and Markups module, not just a terminal or code editor.

integrity.description: The agent generates the file via Slicer, not by manually typing JSON.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is not seen typing coordinates into a text editor or script to generate the output file directly.
The brain MRI images displayed in the slice views are consistent with the 'MRHead' sample data (grayscale anatomical brain scan), not blank or noise.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The average AC-PC distance in the adult human brain is approximately 25.0 mm, with a normal range of 19.0 mm to 32.0 mm. The Anterior Commissure (AC) is located at the anterior wall of the third ventricle. The Posterior Commissure (PC) is located at the junction of the cerebral aqueduct and third ventricle.
